meta.title: The Narrator Who Cannot Be Trusted
meta.theme: how a story lets a reader see past the very narrator telling it

format.format_verified: 2026-07: five sentences, TITA, +3 with no negative marking, consistent in released papers 2016 to 2025; re-verify against each new official paper

traps.description: Both traps at once. Trap B: the intruder is a general definition of fiction sharing narrator and reader, dressed as a thesis. Trap A: a belonging sentence is disguised with a lone present tense address to the reader, luring the shortcut cut.

meta.learning_objective: separate a camouflaged general definition from a costumed belonging sentence using the removal test

source.usage_note: Original composition in CAT register for CAT OS. General narrative theory of unreliable narration; no specific text adapted. Personal study only.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Odd One Out milestone); ODD_MAN_OUT_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: CAT+ both traps. Intruder B is a general definition of fiction; decoy D wears a present tense reader address. Only removing each and reading the remainder separates them. Outlier at B. Owner review pending.

sentences.label: A
sentences.text: The unreliable narrator poses a puzzle: if every word of the story comes through one flawed voice, how can a reader ever learn what that voice will not admit?

sentences.label: B
sentences.text: Fiction, after all, is the art of arranging invented events into a sequence a reader will follow with belief, whatever its distance from fact.

sentences.label: C
sentences.text: The answer is that the writer plants evidence the narrator reports without grasping, small facts whose weight the reader feels and the teller does not.

sentences.label: D
sentences.text: You notice the servant's fear, the guest's quick exit, the letter left unopened, and you assemble from them a truth the narrator never states.

sentences.label: E
sentences.text: So the story tells two versions at once, the one the narrator believes and the one the reader builds behind his back, and the gap between them is the whole design.

builder.prompt: Build the four sentence argument about how a reader sees past an unreliable narrator, from the puzzle to the design. The sentence left out is your answer.

explanation.spine_note: The four sentences argue one specific claim about narrative technique. A poses the puzzle, how can a reader see past a flawed narrator. C answers it, the writer plants evidence the narrator misses. D shows it working, the reader assembling a truth from small facts. E states the design, two versions told at once. Puzzle, answer, illustration, design.

core_roles.label: A
core_roles.why_here: It poses the specific puzzle the whole argument solves, how a reader learns what an unreliable narrator will not admit.

core_roles.label: C
core_roles.why_here: It answers A directly, the writer plants evidence the narrator reports without grasping, and its the answer is names it as the reply.

core_roles.label: D
core_roles.why_here: It makes C concrete, the reader noticing small facts and assembling a truth the narrator never states.

core_roles.label: E
core_roles.why_here: It draws the argument to its point with so, the story telling two versions, the gap between them being the design.

links.explanation: C's the answer stands in for a reply to the exact question A posed, binding the two as puzzle and solution.
C's planted evidence the narrator misses is D's given, and D adds the reader noticing and assembling it.
E opens with so, gathering the planted evidence and the reader's assembly into the conclusion about the double story.

explanation.violation_named: Sentence B is a general definition inside a specific argument. The core solves one focused puzzle about unreliable narration, question, answer, example, design. B defines fiction as a whole, the art of arranging invented events into a believable sequence. It is a textbook generalisation about all fiction, pitched far above the particular technique the paragraph analyses.
explanation.why_it_breaks: The argument needs no definition of fiction in general; it needs the mechanism by which a reader outreads a narrator. B's broad definition connects to nothing in the chain: A's puzzle, C's answer, D's example and E's design never engage the question of what fiction is or its relation to fact. B could open an essay on the novel as such. This paragraph is a tight case about one device, and a definition of the whole art has no seat in it.
explanation.trap_exposed: This item sets both traps. The true intruder B is disguised as a thesis: its after all and its authoritative sweep make a general definition sound like the paragraph's central claim, so a reader keeps it. Meanwhile D is dressed to look odd, breaking into the present tense and addressing you directly, which tempts a shortcut reader to cut it as tonally out of place. The removal test settles it: remove B and the puzzle, answer, example and design stand as a whole; remove D and the argument loses its one concrete illustration. Only one removal leaves a coherent paragraph.

exclusion_analysis.label: A
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: Its opening is a question, not a claim, so a reader deciding early may treat it as a rhetorical frame rather than the load bearing puzzle.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without A there is no puzzle: C's the answer replies to a question the paragraph never asked.

exclusion_analysis.label: C
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: It is the argument's pivot and the most abstract sentence, so before building, its exact seat is the hardest to fix.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without C the puzzle has no solution, and D's noticing illustrates a mechanism the paragraph never named.

exclusion_analysis.label: D
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: This is the planted decoy: its present tense and its direct you make it feel tonally apart from the analytic sentences.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without D the argument states its answer but never shows it working, and E's two versions rest on no concrete case.

exclusion_analysis.label: E
exclusion_analysis.why_tempting: E and B both make broad statements about how fiction works, so a hurried read may hold both and skip the removal test.
exclusion_analysis.what_breaks: Without E the argument analyses the technique but never lands its point, that the gap between the two versions is the design.

explanation.detection_habit: When two sentences both look excludable, remove each in turn and read the four that remain. A fair paragraph survives exactly one of those readings, and the removal that leaves coherence names the true intruder.

mentor.challenge: One sentence defines all of fiction and hides as the thesis. Another wears an odd costume and belongs. Do not choose. Remove each and read what is left.
mentor.takeaway: Two suspects, one test: remove each and read the remainder. Exactly one removal leaves a paragraph standing.
